definition.name: NXstm
definition.doc: An application definition to describe Scanning Tunneling Microscopy (STM).

field.name: definition
field.doc: Name of the definition that is used for the application.

field.name: scan_mode
field.doc: The that is performed. Sometimes under the same experiment setup, both constant height and current are measured.

group.name: experiment_instrument
group.doc: The group explains the instrumentation of the STM experiment as well as the environment setup such as the temperature of the tip and other sensors of the corresponding instruments.

The lock-in amplifier information. The device is being used to extract the signal information (e.g. amplitude, phase) from the input signal.
The input noise signal is passed to the lock-in amplifier, the lock-in amplifier modulates the signal by a reference signal then the modulated signal is passed to the high-pass filter and low-pass filter and cuts the signal around a reference frequency band. After applying the demodulation techniques to the filtered signal, and applying other mathematical tools, the lock-in amplifier extracts the amplitude and phase of the input signal.

field.name: modulation_signal_type
field.doc: This is the signal on which the modulation voltage or current will be added.

field.name: modulation_frequency
field.doc: The frequency of the sine modulation that is used to modulate the signal in lock-in.

group.name: scan_environment
group.doc: The environment information for stm or sts experiment.

field.name: stm_head_temp
field.doc: Temperature of STM head. Note: At least one field from stm_head_temperature, cryo_bottom_temp and cryo_sheild_temp must be provided.

field.name: cryo_bottom_temp
field.doc: Temperature of liquid helium cryostat. Note: At least one field from stm_head_temperature, cryo_bottom_temp and cryo_sheild_temp must be provided.

field.name: cryo_shield_temp
field.doc: Temperature of liquid nitrogen shield. Note: At least one field from stm_head_temperature, cryo_bottom_temp and cryo_sheild_temp.

group.doc: The scan control information like scan region or phase space, type of scan (e.g. mesh, spiral, etc.), and scan speed, etc.

field.name: scan_type
field.doc: The type of scan like mesh, spiral, etc. For STS experiment, the scan type is usually a single-point scan (trajectory scan).

group.name: scan_region
group.doc: The region of the scan area.

field.name: scan_range
field.doc: The range of the scan area.

field.name: scan_offset
field.doc: The offset of the scan area in 2D (X and Y) space.

field.name: scan_angle_N
field.doc: The N (substring) denotes the angle of the scan area with different physical axes.

group.name: mesh_SCAN
group.doc: The mesh scan information for STM experiment.

field.name: scan_speed
field.doc: The speed of the scanner or the tip during the scan.

field.name: scan_time
field.doc: The time taken by the scanner to scan the entire area.

field.name: forward_speed_N
field.doc: The N (substring) denotes the forward speed of the scanner.

field.name: backward_speed_N
field.doc: The N (substring) denotes the backward speed of the scanner.

group.name: SCAN_DATA
group.doc: The data that comes from scanning the area.

group.name: tip_temperature
group.doc: This group stands for the same concept as /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/SENSOR[tip_temperature]

group.name: cryo_temperature
group.doc: This group stands for the same concept as /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/SENSOR[cryo_temperature]

group.name: cryo_shield_temperature
group.doc: This group stands for the same concept as /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/SENSOR[cryo_shield_temperature]

group.name: tip_temperature
group.doc: The temperature of the tip one of the tip, cryo_bottom and cryo_shield temperature may be provided.

group.name: cryo_temperature
group.doc: The temperature of the cryostat.

group.name: cryo_shield_temperature
group.doc: The temperature of the cryo shield.

group.name: current_sensor
group.doc: The sensor information.

field.name: current
field.doc: The tunneling current between tip and sample after application of bias voltage.

field.name: current_offset
field.doc: Offset of the current measurement.

group.name: current_calibration
group.doc: Calibration of the current measurement.

field.name: caliberation_time
field.doc: The time or period is taken by the calibration to setup to acquire the data for the calibration.

field.name: coefficients
field.doc: The coefficients of the calibration.

group.doc: An amplifier information for the input signal (e.g. from tip).

field.name: current_gain
field.doc: Proportional relationship between the probe output voltage and the actual tunneling current when measuring the tunneling current.

group.name: bias_spectroscopy_environment
group.doc: To explain bias (sweep measurement) voltage applied to the sample.

Setup and scan data for continuous measurement of bias-voltage on the subject of experiment vs tunneling current from probe. Data from from this experiment can also be used to calculate the dI/dV spectra.

group.name: piazo_sensor
group.doc: The sensor information for the piezo device.

field.name: x
field.doc: The x position of the piezo. In STS experiment, the piezo stays fixed at x,y and z and the the tunneling current is measured with respect to the bias voltage (sweep voltage).

field.name: y
field.doc: The y position of the piezo.

field.name: z
field.doc: The z position of the piezo.

group.doc: The piezo configuration information like piezoelectric device calibration and material properties.

field.name: 2nd_order_corr_N
field.doc: The N (substring) denotes X and Y directions. If one know them, he/she can enter the 2nd order piezo characteristics to compensate the error for that axis. The following equation shows the interpretation of the 2nd order correction parameters, For the X-piezo: "Ux = 1/cx · X + cxx · X2" with units: "[V] = [V/m] · [m] + [V/m2] · [m2]" where cx is the calibration of the piezo X and cxx is the 2nd order correction. The unit for such the second-order correction is (V/m^2).

field.name: calibration_type
field.doc: The name of the calibration type, sometimes it is called e.g active calibration, passive calibration.

field.name: calibration_coeffecient_N
field.doc: The calibration coefficient is the ratio of the actual distance moved by the piezo to the voltage applied to the piezo. It is also called first-order correction.

field.name: drift_N
field.doc: Set up the settings to enable or disable the drift compensation.

field.name: drift_correction_status
field.doc: Whether the drift has been corrected in case there is a deviation in the drift.

field.name: tilt_N
field.doc: The N (substring) denotes X and Y directions, and for both directions tilt needs to be adjusted according to the actual surface.

field.name: hv_gain_N
field.doc: The N (substring) denotes X or Y or Z. In some systems, there is an HV gain readout feature. For these systems, the HV gain should be automatically adjusted whenever the gain is changed at the high voltage amplifier.

group.doc: The positioner information like the position of the tip, the position of the sample, PID loop feedback etc.

group.name: z_controller
group.doc: The PID controller information for the z-axis.

field.name: z
field.doc: To indicate the relative tip position z between tip and sample. The tip position can also be varied when the z_controller is not running.

field.name: set_point
field.doc: The set point for the z-controller to be fixed and the target value could be height or current.

field.name: controller_name
field.doc: The name of the controller or channels.

field.name: controller_status
field.doc: The status of the controller to say was PID has been used or not.

field.name: tip_lift
field.doc: If the tip is lifted from the stable point.

field.name: switch_off_delay
field.doc: The switch-off delay of the controller from its stable point.

group.name: reproducibility_indicators
group.doc: The group's concepts hold the link to the related concepts that define the reproducibility of the STM experiment.

field.name: current
field.doc: The tunneling current between tip and sample after application of bias voltage. link to the target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/SENSOR[current_sensor]/current

field.name: current_offset
field.doc: The tunneling current between tip and sample after application of bias voltage. link to the target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/SENSOR[current_sensor]/current

field.name: current_gain
field.doc: Proportional relationship between the probe output voltage and the actual tunneling current when measuring the tunneling current. link to the target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/SENSOR[current_sensor]/AMPLIFIER[amplifier]/current_gain

group.name: bias_sweep
group.doc: Link to target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/ENVIONMENT[bias_spectroscopy_environment]/BIAS_SPECTROSCOPY[bias_spectroscopy]/BIAS_SWEEP[bias_sweep]

field.name: modulation_signal_type
field.doc: This is the signal on which the modulation voltage or current will be added. link to the target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/LOCKIN[lockin]/modulation_signal_type

field.name: modulation_frequency
field.doc: The frequency of the sine modulation that is used to modulate the signal in lock-in. link to the target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/LOCKIN[lockin]/modulation_frequency

group.name: resolution_indicators
group.doc: The group's concepts hold the link to the related concepts that define the resolution of the STM experiment.

group.name: stm_head_temp
group.doc: Link to target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/SENSOR[tip_temperature]

group.name: cryo_bottom_temp
group.doc: Link to target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/SENSOR[cryo_bottom_temperature]

group.name: cryo_shield_temp
group.doc: Link to target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/SENSOR[cryo_shield_temperature]

group.name: bias_sweep
group.doc: Link to target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/ENVIONMENT[bias_spectroscopy_environment]/BIAS_SPECTROSCOPY[bias_spectroscopy]/BIAS_SWEEP[bias_sweep]

field.name: modulation_signal_type
field.doc: This is the signal on which the modulation voltage or current will be added. link to the target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/LOCKIN[lockin]/modulation_signal_type

field.name: modulation_frequency
field.doc: The frequency of the sine modulation that is used to modulate the signal in lock-in. link to the target: /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/LOCKIN[lockin]/modulation_frequency
